item.title: Things your smart glasses can do
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on smart glasses, Meta.

item.title: The World Cup’s quiet winner: Short-term rentals
item.description: Thousands of homeowners in host cities cashed in on the tournament using platforms like Airbnb.

item.title: Why America’s best minds should serve
item.description: Elite universities and military service, AI individuality and Hains Point, in readers' eyes.

item.title: How should the court address discrimination?
item.description: Supreme Court recent civil rights decisions and the Lafayette Square fence, in readers' eyes.

item.title: Why doesn’t Europe have AC?
item.description: Is AC a luxury or a necessity? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Cato Institute research fellow Chelsea Follett joins James Hohmann to discuss Europe's cultural and regulatory resistance to air conditioning. The conversation explores the dangers of modern "degrowthism" and climate fatalism, contrasting them with a solutions-oriented, pro-technology mindset.

item.title: The greatest editorial cartoonist? The case for Pat Oliphant.
item.description: No one who held power was safe from his pen.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s critics swarm to his light. But they don’t know him.
item.description: The longtime senator from South Carolina chose action over irrelevance.

item.title: Richard Branson: What earthquakes and prisons show about Venezuela under Trump
item.description: Getting political prisoners freed would demonstrate that Trump's end goals are freedom and democracy.

item.title: Lonely Gen Z needed this wake-up call
item.description:
Gen Z has been told a lot of things about loneliness: that it’s caused by phones, social media, remote work, dating apps, the pandemic, the decline of third places and a culture that makes staying home easier than ever. But what if the more uncomfortable truth is that loneliness is also something people have to actively fight?
Host Megan McArdle talks with Wall Street Journal editor Emma Camp about her essay “Loneliness Is for Cowards,” why so many young people seem afraid of embarrassment and rejection, and how to start making real friends again.

item.title: Frozen
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on ICE-related deaths, Trump administration.

item.title: Does the right have a cancel culture problem?
item.description: Is higher education too powerful to ignore? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Jason Willick sits down with Greg Lukianoff, president and CEO of the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression, to unpack the escalating war over campus free expression, Title IX and the Trump administration's tactics targeting Harvard. They also dive into the psychology of modern censorship and peer into the future of AI, warning why elite control over algorithmic outputs might be the ultimate threat to human knowledge.

item.title: Erica Schwartz’s confirmation hearing shows her impossible task at CDC
item.description: She inspired little confidence that she would serve as an effective check on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

item.title: ‘Throw it all out’: My parents’ stuff is more than clutter
item.description: Mom’s samovar? Dad’s chess set? I can’t bear to part with them.
